Please file legal authority for distribution to Terry A. Lam and for not distributing according to Decedent's Will. d. The Petitioner's request for order gives alternatives in the distribution. The petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed. (LRSF 14.67 L.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GENETTE TRAMMELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Of Petitioner Donald Trammell By And Through His Gal Clinton Killians Memorandum In Support Of Order Directing Compliance With Subpoena Duces Tecum
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Before the court is Petitioner Donald Trammell's Motion to Compel Compliance with Subpoena [sic] Duces Tecum. The motion is DENIED. The motion is denied as to all parties because Petitioner failed to identify the parties against whom the motion was brought, a fundamental component of due process. Further, some parties against whom the motion appears to be brought responded to the discovery and Petitioner failed to provide a separate statement, as required by CRC 3.1345. The motion is denied as to all third parties (to the extent it's brought against them). There is no showing that the deposition subpoenas, issued under CCP section 2020.410, were accompanied by either the consumer's written authorization to release personal records, or proof of service of a notice of privacy rights. (CCP sections 1985.3(c) and 1985.6(c), and CCP section 2020.410.) Failure to comply with any of the requirements under CCP section 1985.3 invalidates the service, and the custodian is under no duty to produce the records sought by the subpoena. (CCP section 1985.3(k).) There is no showing on the record that all of the eighteen subpoenaed parties were properly served the subpoenas, either. There is, further, no showing that the nonparty deponents were served notice of this motion in compliance with CRC 3.1346. As to Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc., Caroline Ingeborn and Gustav Lindqvist, the motion is denied. The motion is untimely. Insofar as these respondents served objections to an unspecified portion of the eighteen subpoenas now at issue, the statutory 20-day period in which to bring a motion to compel responses began to run upon service of the objections in May 2019 and has since run. The 60-day statutory period in which to bring a motion to compel response to a deposition subpoena has also run (CCP sections 1987.1, 2025.480(b). Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc.'s request for sanctions under CCP section 2023.020 on grounds of Petitioner's failure to meet and confer before filing this motion is GRANTED. The preponderance of the evidence shows that Petitioner failed to make a timely, reasonable and good faith effort to meet-and-confer to resolve informally issues raised by the subpoena to Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc. (See CCP section 2023.020 [court "shall" order party or counsel who fails to confer as required to pay the reasonable expenses, including attorney's fees, incurred by anyone as a result of that conduct].) Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc. are awarded attorney's fees of $2,525, the amount the court finds reasonable. Kilby Stenkamp and Vanguard Properties, Inc. are ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on September 29, 2020. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate.

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO CONTRA COSTA CO.** IN THE MATTER OF THE ALFONSO PORTILLO IRREVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner Elaine Jennings'S Petition: To Determine The Existence Of Alfonso Portillo Irrevocable Trust, Dated December 18, 2017; To Impose Constructi
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The Court to determine if the case can be transferred based on a stipulation without a petition to transfer venue. Please review the 6th amended general order on the court's website re the strong preference for all attorneys and parties to appear remotely as a result of the COVID-19 emergency and for information on appearing by CourtCall or via Zoom. (A 7th amended general order is imminent. Parties should check the Court's website closer to the hearing date for further instructions regarding remote appearance.) For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOK CHURCH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 10/28/20. A proposed personal representative is required to sign and verify his own pleadings and any supplement or amendment thereto. [CRC 7.103(c).] (The attorney should not be declaring anything.) The petitioner's verification of the attorney's declaration filed on 9/2/20 is incomplete (not dated). The change in the petition caption is an amendment. If a brother survived the decedent, item 6f is not the first box that applies. Not all beneficiaries named in the will are listed in item 8; nor did those beneficiaries receive notice. The only proper form of notice for an initial petition is the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate. The proof of service filed on 8/14/20 is incomplete at item 4. "Beneficiary named in will" is not a relationship.
